summary: Addresses review #5 feedback from greptile-apps[bot]: (1) added docstring warning about ConfigMap accumulation during owner lifetime, advising reconcilers to prune obsolete ConfigMaps; (2) fixed hash collision vulnerability by switching from naive key=value\n encoding to length-prefixed format (len:key=len:value\n), making entries self-delimiting regardless of embedded newlines or '=' in values; (3) added test TestCreateImmutableConfigMap_newlineInValueIsUnambiguous proving the encoding distinguishes values with embedded newlines from separate key-value pairs. The third external feedback item (EnsureCertificate return type in docs) was already correct — docs/reference/kubernetes-packages.md already shows (bool, error).

tests_checklist.item: All unit tests pass (go test ./internal/common/config/... — 0 failures)
New test covers the specific hash collision scenario (embedded newlines)
Existing tests unchanged and still pass (idempotent, deterministic, differentData, rejectsUnownedExisting)
Test uses separate fake clients to avoid cross-contamination (c1, c2)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing gomega test patterns (NewGomegaWithT, g.Expect)
CC-0005 feature tag present in test comment
Docstring update is accurate and actionable
Comment in hash computation explains the encoding rationale
No dead code or unnecessary changes

security_checklist.item: Hash encoding is now unambiguous — no collision vectors from embedded delimiters
No hardcoded secrets or sensitive data

architecture_checklist.item: Change is minimal and focused — only the hash encoding and docstring
No scope creep beyond the external review feedback

fail_fast_checklist.item: Error paths unchanged and still correct

defensive_checklist.item: Length-prefixed encoding handles all edge cases (empty keys, empty values, newlines, '=' in values)

review_steps.1_INTENT: Address 3 items from greptile-apps[bot] review #5: docstring warning, hash collision fix, docs return type
review_steps.2_SCOPE: Changes limited to config.go (docstring + hash encoding) and config_test.go (new collision test). No scope creep.
review_steps.3_VERIFICATION: go vet pass, golangci-lint 0 issues, go test all pass
review_steps.4_PATTERNS: Test follows established gomega/NewGomegaWithT pattern, CC-0005 tag convention maintained
review_steps.5_TESTS: New test directly proves the collision scenario described in the review. Uses two separate fake clients with distinct data that would collide under naive encoding.
review_steps.6_SECURITY: Hash collision vulnerability fixed — length-prefixed encoding eliminates delimiter ambiguity
review_steps.7_COMPLEXITY: Minimal change: single fmt.Fprintf format string update. Simplest possible fix.
review_steps.8_COMPLETENESS: All 3 external feedback items addressed: (1) docstring added, (2) encoding fixed with test, (3) docs already correct
